name: Route of the Lanceros — circular on the Camino Primitivo
name_original: Ruta de los Lanceros — circular en el Camino Primitivo
region_intro: Hiking around Las Regueras offers access to a landscape characterized by the meanders of the Nora River, dense forests, and traditional Asturian villages. The region's name, meaning "the streams," reflects the abundance of water currents that cross its terrain. This area features significant riverside vegetation, including white willows, and provides a refuge for migratory bird species. The terrain includes varied elevations and rural scenery, suitable for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Las Regueras The most popular hiking route is Route of the Lanceros — circular on the Camino Primitivo, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route partly coincides with the Camino de Santiago and offers views of the Nora River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñaflor to Grado on the Camino Primitivo, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This trail follows a section of the historic pilgrimage route, connecting rural areas. Local hikers also love the Orchard Path – View of the Nalón River loop from Vega de Anzo/Veiga D'Anzu, a 13.1 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around Las Regueras is defined by river meanders, diverse forests, and traditional rural scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Las Regueras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Las Regueras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Las Regueras?
A: Las Regueras offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 215 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Las Regueras?
A: Yes, Las Regueras has 72 easy hiking routes, perfect for beginners or families. A notable option is the &lt;strong&gt;Senda Verde El Pedreru - San Pedro de Nora&lt;/strong&gt;, an easy 4-kilometer path suitable for both hiking and cycling, featuring rest areas and leading to the surroundings of the Pre-Romanesque Church of San Pedro de Nora.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Las Regueras?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Las Regueras are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048069584/route-of-the-lanceros-circular-on-the-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Lanceros — circular on the Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 10 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24654023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana de Premoño – Rest Area with Stone Bench loop from Las Regueras&lt;/a&gt; offers another moderate circular option.

Q: What are some options for longer distance hikes in Las Regueras?
A: For those seeking longer treks, the region offers several routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048070304/penaflor-to-grado-on-the-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor to Grado on the Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.8 km path following a section of the historic pilgrimage route. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orchard Path – View of the Nalón River loop from Vega de Anzo/Veiga D'Anzu&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 21 km.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit while hiking in Las Regueras?
A: Yes, you can discover waterfalls in the area. The &lt;strong&gt;Cascada de La Maserona&lt;/strong&gt; is a notable waterfall located in the Alto de La Collada, offering a refreshing spot and even the possibility for a swim during summer months.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Las Regueras is rich in landmarks. You can encounter the Pre-Romanesque Church of San Pedro de Nora, Roman Baths of Valduno, and historical bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1137104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte de Galiñeiro&lt;/a&gt; over the Nora River. The Nora River meanders themselves are a Natural Monument, offering impressive riverside vegetation and birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering scenic panoramas in Las Regueras?
A: Yes, the region provides several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Ruta de los Lanceros&lt;/strong&gt; offers access to the "Mirador de los Meandros," providing unique perspectives of the Nora River's curves. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peñona&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight in the broader area offering expansive views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Las Regueras?
A: Las Regueras is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while summer provides opportunities for refreshing dips in spots like Cascada de La Maserona. Winter can also be beautiful, though some trails might be wetter.

Q: Is it possible to hike parts of the Camino Primitivo (Primitive Way of St. James) through Las Regueras?
A: Yes, a significant portion of the historic Camino Primitivo passes directly through Las Regueras. This allows hikers to experience a segment of this ancient pilgrimage route, sharing paths with pilgrims and enjoying the captivating landscapes. Pilgrim refuges are available in El Escamplero and La Portalada.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Las Regueras?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's pure and rural essence, the diverse natural landscapes including the Nora River meanders, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any prehistoric sites or caves to explore in the region?
A: Yes, Las Regueras is home to several prehistoric caves, including Cueva Oscura de Ania, Cueva de La Paloma, Cueva de Sofoxó, and Cueva de Las Mestas. These sites are of significant archaeological importance, offering a glimpse into the region's ancient past with findings of instruments and rock carvings.

Q: Are there any routes that showcase the traditional architecture of Las Regueras?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Ruta de los Palacios&lt;/strong&gt; is a 14-kilometer route that highlights architectural marvels. Along this trail, you can see structures such as the Palace of Bolgues, the Palace of Ardaxe, and the Hermitage of Santa Ana de Premoño, providing insight into the region's historical heritage.
tips: In the small population center of Valsera is the chapel of Nuestra Señora de Fátima, built in the fifties on the remains of the church destroyed in a fire in 1930. It has tables for rest and a fountain
An old restored water mill. It is located in an idyllic corner of Las Regueras.
The views are impressive. Natural monument viewpoint from which you can see the course of the Nora river in the vicinity of Priañes. The views are impressive, especially on a clear day.
Beautiful landscape! There is a picnic area with barbecues and picnic tables in the middle of the forest, perfect for sunny days.
